hero.title:
Markdown to production docs.
One command. Zero config.
hero.sub:
Static HTML for SEO. SPA-speed navigation. AI-ready by default.
No React. No framework lock-in. No learning curve.
feature.zeroconfig.label: Getting Started
feature.zeroconfig.title: Zero config to start. Full control when you need it.
feature.zeroconfig.desc: Run one command. Get a production docs site. Customize later with a simple config file — no boilerplate, no framework lock-in.
feature.zeroconfig.li1: Single command setup with npx docmd init
feature.zeroconfig.li3: Works with any existing markdown structure
feature.containers.label: Content
feature.containers.title: Rich layouts without HTML.
feature.containers.desc: Write callouts, tabs, cards, and more using native Markdown container syntax. Complex layouts, zero HTML required.
feature.containers.li4: Icon support in all container types
feature.search.label: Search
feature.search.title: Instant search. Zero API keys. 100% offline.
feature.search.desc: Full-text fuzzy search built into every site. No cloud dependencies, no API costs. Works completely offline with per-locale indexes for multilingual docs.
feature.ai.label: AI Native
feature.ai.title: Built for the AI era.
feature.ai.desc: Every docmd site ships with llms.txt and llms-full.txt context streams. Semantic containers give AI agents high-fidelity signals about your documentation structure.
feature.ai.li2: Semantic HTML containers for better parsing
feature.ai.li3: AI bot crawl controls via SEO plugin
feature.extras.label: Production Ready
feature.extras.title: i18n, themes, versioning, and one-command deploy.
feature.extras.desc: Everything you need for production documentation — multi-language support, light and dark themes, version switching, and deployment configs for Docker, Nginx, and Caddy.
feature.extras.li2: Light & dark themes with custom CSS
plugins.title: Everything included.
plugins.desc: Core plugins ship by default. Add optional ones when you need them.
built.title: Built for every documentation need.
built.desc: From open-source READMEs to enterprise knowledge bases.
faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.sub: Quick answers to common questions about docmd.
faq.a1: docmd is an open-source, zero-config documentation engine that converts Markdown files into production-ready documentation sites. It generates static HTML for SEO while providing SPA-like navigation for speed. Read the quick start guide →
faq.q2: How is it different from Docusaurus or VitePress?
faq.a2: docmd requires zero configuration — no React, no Vue, no framework knowledge. It produces standalone HTML with <20kb client JS, scores Lighthouse 100, and includes search, SEO, AI context (llms.txt), and i18n without setup. See the full comparison →
faq.q3: Do I need React or any framework?
faq.a3: No. docmd is framework-free. You write Markdown, run one command, and get a production site. Plain HTML + minimal vanilla JS. No bundlers, no framework lock-in.
faq.q4: How does the AI-ready feature work?
faq.a4: docmd auto-generates llms.txt and llms-full.txt files that give AI models structured context about your docs. Combined with semantic HTML containers, AI agents navigate your documentation with high fidelity. Learn about the LLMs plugin →
faq.a5: Yes. Completely free and open source under MIT License. All core plugins are included by default. View on GitHub →
faq.q6: Can I use it with my existing Markdown files?
faq.a6: Yes. Point docmd at any directory of Markdown files. It supports standard Markdown, frontmatter, and adds container syntax (callouts, tabs, cards) on top. Markdown syntax reference →
faq.a7: Yes. Native i18n with locale-first URLs, per-locale search indexes, and translated UI strings. Configure with a simple locales array. i18n configuration →
ask.title: Still deciding?
ask.sub: Ask your favorite AI about docmd, or read the docs yourself.
cta.title: Ready to write better docs?
cta.desc: Join the developers building fast, beautiful documentation with docmd.
